swiss-mono-index.name: Swiss Mono Index
swiss-mono-index.brief: A precision-indexed portfolio or catalog site where strict column grids and monospaced type work together — labels, metadata, and category markers feel like a living specification document. The board's heaviest channels combine into a site that is rigorous, cold, and quietly authoritative. White fields, hairline rules, and character-grid spacing leave no room for decoration.
notes: No decorative or display typefaces — grotesk and mono only, hierarchy via size and weight
No gradients, color fields, or atmospheric backgrounds — ground must stay white or light neutral
No overlapping or collaged elements — all content crops flush to grid columns

monolith-grid.name: Monolith Grid
monolith-grid.brief: A brand or studio landing page where a single dominant wordmark commands the white field, supported by a strict underlying grid that keeps any secondary content — metadata, a navigation index, a tagline — tightly ordered and subordinate. The board's cream-to-white ground and black type mass anchor the identity; red appears as one restrained accent at most.
notes: One accent color maximum — red (#ff3300) used sparingly if at all; never a multi-hue palette
No photography as hero and no decorative background textures — type IS the visual
No centered hero compositions or asymmetric blob layouts — grid discipline must hold even around the wordmark

brutalist-case-doc.name: Brutalist Case Doc
brutalist-case-doc.brief: A work or process showcase that presents documentation with aggressive graphic weight — heavy grotesk at large scale structures each section while underlying logic is strictly evidentiary: process screens, artifacts, and sequenced content blocks. The board's black-and-white ground with a single maximum-chroma red hit makes the site feel like a zine and a report simultaneously.
notes: No serif typefaces or rounded UI — heavy grotesk only, used as graphic mass
No smooth eased transitions or theatrical entrances — cuts are abrupt; scroll reveals are structural
No high-chroma color fields or atmospheric treatments — red is the single accent, used once with full commitment

warm-editorial-meta.name: Warm Meta Editorial
warm-editorial-meta.brief: A writing-forward or studio journal site where cream and blush grounds carry literary-spaced editorial type, while monospaced labels, datestamps, and category tags introduce a quiet system layer — warmth and precision held in careful tension. The board's pale yellow (#ffffcc), pink tones (#ffcccc, #ff66cc), and off-white palette ground this in softness without tipping into generic pastel.
notes: No cold white or dark backgrounds — ground must stay warm cream or blush at all times
No high-chroma neon accents — pink and yellow used at low saturation; #ff66cc only as a restrained label accent
No expressive motion or organic shapes — mono metadata labels must feel measured and grid-anchored even within the warm editorial layout
